book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
Extracts from the Acts. Session VIII.
title: Extracts from the Acts. Session VIII.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: Let George, the most holy archbishop of this our God-preserved city, and let Macarius, the venerable archbishop of Antioch, and let the synod subject to them [i.e., their suffragans] say, if they submit to the force (εἰ στοιχοῦσι τῇ δυνάμει) of the suggestions sent by the most holy Agatho Pope of Old Rome and by his Synod.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: I have diligently examined the whole force of the suggestions sent to your most pious Fortitude, as well by Agatho, the most holy Pope of Old Rome, as by his synod, and I have scrutinized the works of the holy and approved Fathers, which are laid up in my venerable patriarchate, and I have found that all the testimonies of the holy and accepted Fathers, which are contained in those suggestions agree with, and in no particular differ from, the holy and accepted Fathers.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: Therefore I give my submission to them and thus I profess and believe.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: And we, most pious Lord, accepting the teaching of the suggestion sent to your most gentle Fortitude by the most holy and blessed Agatho, Pope of Old Rome, and of that other suggestion which was adopted by the council subject to him, and following the sense therein contained, so we are minded, so we profess, and so we believe that in our one Lord Jesus Christ, our true God, there are two natures unconfusedly, unchangeably, undividedly, and two natural wills and two natural operations; and all who have taught, and who now say, that there is but one will and one operation in the two natures of our one Lord Jesus Christ our true God, we anathematize.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: Let Macarius, the Venerable Archbishop of Antioch, who has now heard what has been said by this holy and Ecumenical Synod [demanding the expression of his faith], answer what seemeth him good.

verses.book: The Sixth Ecumenical Council. The Third Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: I do not say that there are two wills or two operations in the dispensation of the incarnation of our Lord Jesus Christ, but one will and one theandric operation.
